# Io

> A small, prototype-based programming language — now targeting WASM/WASI.

## Documentation

- [Documentation](/docs/): Guides, tutorial, language reference, and design notes.
- [Future Work](/docs/Future%20Work/): Design notes and proposals for upcoming changes.
- [Exceptions](/docs/Future%20Work/Exceptions/): Design notes on the current exception system and proposed improvements.
- [Guide](/docs/Guide/): Comprehensive language guide: syntax, messages, objects, control flow, concurrency, and more.
- [Reference](/docs/Reference/)
- [Block](/docs/Reference/Block/): Blocks are anonymous functions (messages with their own locals object). They are typically used to represent object methods.
- [Call](/docs/Reference/Call/): Call stores slots related to activation.
- [CFunction](/docs/Reference/CFunction/)
- [Collector](/docs/Reference/Collector/)
- [Compiler](/docs/Reference/Compiler/): Contains methods related to the compiling code.
- [Continuation](/docs/Reference/Continuation/): A first-class continuation captured by callcc. Represents a resumable snapshot of execution state; invoking it re-enters the captured context.
- [Coroutine](/docs/Reference/Coroutine/): Object wrapper for an Io coroutine. Now implemented using frame-based evaluation (no platform-specific assembly).
- [Date](/docs/Reference/Date/): An absolute point in time, represented as seconds since the epoch.
- [Debugger](/docs/Reference/Debugger/): Contains methods related to the IoVM debugger.
- [Directory](/docs/Reference/Directory/)
- [DirectoryCollector](/docs/Reference/DirectoryCollector/): An object to collect multiple UnitTests defined in *Test.io files within a given directory (System launchPath directory by default).
- [Duration](/docs/Reference/Duration/): A container for a duration of time.
- [Error](/docs/Reference/Error/): An object that contains error information and flow control based on errors.
- [EvalFrame](/docs/Reference/EvalFrame/): Returns a human-readable description of this frame, including the message name, state, and source location.
- [Exception](/docs/Reference/Exception/)
- [false](/docs/Reference/false/): The false singleton — Io's representation of boolean falsity.
- [File](/docs/Reference/File/)
- [FileCollector](/docs/Reference/FileCollector/): An object to collect multiple UnitTests defined in the current file.
- [FileImporter](/docs/Reference/FileImporter/): An Importer for local source files.
- [Importer](/docs/Reference/Importer/): A simple search path based auto-importer.
- [List](/docs/Reference/List/): A mutable array of values. The first index is 0.
- [Map](/docs/Reference/Map/): A key/value dictionary appropriate for holding large key/value collections.
- [Message](/docs/Reference/Message/)
- [MutableSequence](/docs/Reference/MutableSequence/): A Sequence whose contents can be modified in place. MutableSequence is the receiver for the mutating methods of Sequence (append, insert, remove, etc.).
- [nil](/docs/Reference/nil/): nil is a singleton object that is used as a placeholder and to mean false in Io.
- [Number](/docs/Reference/Number/): A container for a double (a 64bit floating point number on most platforms).
- [Object](/docs/Reference/Object/)
- [Path](/docs/Reference/Path/): Path manipulation: joining, splitting, absolute/relative conversion, and tilde expansion.
- [Profiler](/docs/Reference/Profiler/): Basic support for profiling Io code execution.
- [Sandbox](/docs/Reference/Sandbox/): Sandbox can be used to run separate instances of Io within the same process.
- [Scheduler](/docs/Reference/Scheduler/): Io's coroutine scheduler.
- [Sequence](/docs/Reference/Sequence/)
- [State](/docs/Reference/State/)
- [System](/docs/Reference/System/): Contains methods related to the IoVM.
- [TestRunner](/docs/Reference/TestRunner/): Core testing object responsible for running tests, collected by UnitTests and TestSuites.
- [true](/docs/Reference/true/): The true singleton — Io's representation of boolean truth.
- [UnitTest](/docs/Reference/UnitTest/): An object for organizing and running unit tests validated by assertions.
- [WeakLink](/docs/Reference/WeakLink/)
- [Stackless](/docs/Stackless/): Io's iterative evaluator, continuations, and coroutines.
- [Stackless VM Examples](/docs/Stackless/Examples/)
- [Stackless Evaluator Report](/docs/Stackless/Report/)
- [Style Guide](/docs/Style%20Guide/): How to write idiomatic Io — naming, formatting, comments, and the patterns used throughout the standard library.
- [Tutorial](/docs/Tutorial/): A short, example-driven tour of Io for folks who already know how to program. Each section is a quick REPL session.
- [WASM](/docs/WASM/): Running Io in the browser via WebAssembly.
- [Browser Target](/docs/WASM/Browser%20Target/): Io runs in the browser as a WebAssembly module. The browser build compiles the full VM into a WASM reactor module that JS loads and drives via exported functions.
- [DOM Interop](/docs/WASM/DOM%20Interop/): Io code running in the browser can query, create, and manipulate HTML elements through the DOM object and Element instances.
